Tn-Seq Pipeline

library(tidyverse)
library(Biostrings)
library(DESeq2)
library(mclust)

This pipeline is based on previously work published by Dr. Ibberson’s (Ibberson et al., 2017; Lewin et al., 2019). The code in this notebook is adapted with some modifications to fit our data and analysis needs. The original resources can be found here:

  • Generating a TA sites file
  • Tn-seq Mapping
  • Essential Gene Analysis
  • Additional scripts can be found here, including some dependency scripts like the TnGeneBin.pl script for binning reads into genes as part of the essential gene analysis.
FOLDER_PATH <- Sys.getenv("BOX_PATH")
TnSeq_folder <- file.path(FOLDER_PATH, "Tn-Seq")

Reference genome and TA sites

We used Staphylococcus aureus subsp. aureus USA300_FPR3757 GCF_000013465.1 as reference genome. The assembly was downloaded from NCBI on 12/21/23. Curated versions of the assembly were manually created containing only the main chromosome NC_007793.1 (the 3 plasmids were removed) and saved as Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta and Sau_USA300_FPR3757.gff in data/TnSeq/RefSeqs.

gff_pfx = "Sau_USA300_FPR3757"
gff_dir = file.path(TnSeq_folder, "RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757.gff")
genome_dir <- file.path(TnSeq_folder,"RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta")

Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta was indexed for use with bowtie2 later in the pipeline

#conda activate Tn-Seq
bowtie2-build data/TnSeq/RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta data/TnSeq/RefSeqs/USA300

For Mariner transposons, which only insert in TA sites, it is beneficial to identify all of the TA sites and restrict to these sites for downstream analysis. To generate a .csv file of all of the TA sites in a genome, the following code was used:

genome <- readDNAStringSet(genome_dir)
ta_sites <- vmatchPattern("TA", genome)
ta_vect <- unname(unlist(start(ta_sites)))
ta_df <- data_frame(sites = ta_vect)
write.csv(ta_df$sites, file.path(TnSeq_folder, "RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_TAsites.csv"))

Tn-seq Mapping

This section is based in the TnSeq2.sh script. This section processes the raw fastq files, maps the reads to the reference genome, and tallies the mapping results to identify insertion sites and read counts per site.

myPath="data/TnSeq/"

mkdir -p $myPath/logs
mkdir -p $myPath/bam
mkdir -p $myPath/sites
mkdir -p $myPath/genes

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.fastq.gz; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "Initializing TnSeq analysis on $PREFIX"
    echo "TnSeq processing stats for $PREFIX" > $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    echo "Number of raw reads: " >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    zcat $file | grep -c '^@' >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done

Trimming of IRs & Adaptors

This code chunk removes adapters by removing every sequence before the IR sequence using cutadapt (Martin, 2011). The number of reads post-trimming is logged for each sample. The -m 20 option ensures that only reads of at least 20 bp after trimming are retained, which helps to filter out very short reads that may not be informative for mapping.

myPath="data/TnSeq/"

#conda activate Tn-Seq
IR="GCCAACCTGTTA"

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.fastq.gz; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "$PREFIX: Removing primer and IR sequences..."
    cutadapt -g $IR -m 20 --discard-untrimmed -o $myPath/fastq/$PREFIX.trim.fastq $file > $myPath/logs/$PREFIX.cutadapt_log.txt
    echo "Number of post-trimmed reads: " >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    grep -c '^@' $myPath/fastq/$PREFIX.trim.fastq >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done

Some of the samples were resequenced, so we had to concatenate the trim files for the original and resequenced samples before moving forward with the analysis.

myPath="data/TnSeq/"

cd $myPath/fastq
cat 5772_021_S19_R1_001.trim.fastq 5772_021reseq_S1_R1_001.trim.fastq > 5772_021_S01_R1_001.trim.fastq
cat 5772_022_S20_R1_001.trim.fastq 5772_022reseq_S2_R1_001.trim.fastq > 5772_022_S00_R1_001.trim.fastq
cat 5772_027_S1_R1_001.trim.fastq 5772_027_S61_R1_001.trim.fastq > 5772_027_S00_R1_001.trim.fastq

Mapping to reference genome

This code chunk maps the reads to the reference genome using Bowtie2 (Langmead and Salzberg, 2012; Langmead et al., 2018) and processes the resulting SAM files with samtools (Danecek et al., 2021) to generate sorted BAM files containing only mapped, primary alignments. The mapping report from Bowtie2 and the number of mapped reads are logged for each sample.

myPath="data/TnSeq/"
GENOME=$myPath"RefSeqs/USA300"

#conda activate Tn-Seq

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.trim.fastq; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "$PREFIX: Mapping with Bowtie2..."
    echo "Bowtie2 report:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    bowtie2 --end-to-end -p 16 -a -x $GENOME -U $myPath/fastq/$PREFIX.trim.fastq 2>> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt | \
    samtools view -b -F 4 -F 2048 | samtools sort -@ 16 -o $myPath/bam/$PREFIX-mapped.bam
    echo "Number of mapped reads:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    samtools view -c $myPath/bam/$PREFIX-mapped.bam >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done

Tallying mapping results

This code chunk processes the mapped BAM files to identify unique insertion sites and count the number of reads mapping to each site. The results are saved in text files for each sample, and summary statistics about the number of insertion sites and the most frequent sites are logged.

myPath="data/TnSeq/"

#conda activate Tn-Seq

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.trim.fastq; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "$PREFIX: Tallying mapping results..."
    samtools view -F 256 $myPath/bam/$PREFIX-mapped.bam | \
        awk '{if(($2) != 16) print $4; else print $4+length($10)}' | \
        sort | uniq -c | sort -k1nr \
        > $myPath/sites/$PREFIX-sites.txt
    echo "Number of insertion sites identified:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    wc -l $myPath/sites/$PREFIX-sites.txt >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    echo "Most frequent sites:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    head -10 $myPath/sites/$PREFIX-sites.txt >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done

Essential Gene Analysis

This section is based on the Essential Gene Analysis script. The code in this section reads in the site counts generated in the previous section, generates expected datasets with the same number of sites and reads, bins the sites into genes using the TnGeneBin.pl script, and performs DESeq2 analysis to identify essential genes.

Counting reads per site and generating expected datasets

We manually made subfolders to separates the sites files for each sample type (input and lac) so that we could run the following code separately for each sample type. The code chunk below is where you select which sample type to run the analysis on. You will need to update the ctrl_pfx variable based on the sample type you are running (e.g. “input” or “lac”) as this is used in the naming of output files and summary stats.

# Run this code chunk separately for each sample type, updating the mydata and ctrl_pfx variables accordingly
mydata <- file.path(TnSeq_folder, "sites/sites_input")
ctrl_pfx = paste(gff_pfx, "input", sep = "_") #update name based on sample type

#mydata <- file.path(TnSeq_folder, "sites/sites_lac")
#ctrl_pfx = paste(gff_pfx, "lac", sep = "_") #update name based on sample type

This code chunk reads in the site counts for each sample, generates expected datasets with the same number of sites and reads, and outputs the read counts per site and gene boundaries for the binning script. It also outputs summary statistics about the number of sites and reads to a text file.

filenames <- dir(mydata, pattern = ".*-sites.txt", full.names = TRUE)
conditions <- sub("-sites.txt", "", filenames)

ctrl_reps = length(filenames)
num_expected = 100
out_pfx = paste(ctrl_pfx, "EssentialGenes_100", sep = "_")
to_trim = 0

# create summary stats file to record summary info

write(out_pfx, file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""))
write(paste( "\n", "input files:", sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(filenames, file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(paste( "\n", "gff: ", gff_pfx, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(paste("\n", "to trim: ", to_trim, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(paste("number of expected datasets: ", num_expected, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)

# Read in sites files
        sites <- data.frame(Pos = c(0)) 
        for (i in 1:length(conditions)) {
                newsites <- read.table(paste(paste(conditions[i], sep = "/"), "sites.txt", sep = "-")) 
                colnames(newsites) <- c(paste("V", i, sep = ""), "Pos")
                newsites <- arrange(newsites,Pos)
                sites <- merge(sites, newsites, all = T) 
        }
        sites <- tail(sites, n = -1)
        sites[is.na(sites)] <- 0

        #output number of total and shared sites across samples. Need to edit based on number of samples.
        sites_total <- sites %>% mutate(numreps = (V1 > 0) + (V2 > 0)) %>% filter(numreps >= 1)
        write(paste("number of sites:", nrow(sites_total)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        sites_shared <- sites %>% mutate(numreps = (V1 > 0) + (V2 > 0)) %>% filter(numreps >= 2)
        write(paste("number of sites shared by all samples:", nrow(sites_shared)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)

        # Normalize data by reads/site
        colData <- data.frame(c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps)), condition = rep("treated", ctrl_reps))
        sitescds <- sites[,2:length(sites)] %>% round %>% DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(colData = colData, design = ~ 1)
        sitescds <- estimateSizeFactors(sitescds)
        #Output the normalized counts
        counts.norm <- counts(sitescds, normalized = F)
        rownames(counts.norm) <- sites$Pos

        # Initialize the list of genes, determine genome length
        #can add column names if your gff has extra columns
        gff <- read.delim(gff_dir, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, skip = 9, header = FALSE, col.names = c("seqname", "source", "feature", "start", "end", "score", "strand", "frame", "att"))
        #change this value if your gff file contains anything besides genes that you want to read in
        gff <- gff[(gff$feature == "gene"),]

        # Initialize read counts per gene and number of independent Tn sites per gene
        # Generate pseudo-datasets with the same number of insertion sites and total reads mapping to those sites, randomly distributed across the genome
        counts.df <- data.frame(counts.norm)
        counts.df$Pos <- as.numeric(rownames(counts.df))
        numreads <- sum(counts.norm)/ctrl_reps
        numsites <- length(which(counts.norm > 0))/ctrl_reps
        
        #generates a new sites data frame and calculates the mean counts for each site in a new column. This column (containing the mean) is then used to sample from to generate the expected datasets.
        sites2 <- sites[2:(ctrl_reps + 1)]
        sites2$mean <- rowMeans(sites2) %>% round

        #For mariner transposon, define Possible_sites
        Possible_sites <- read_csv(paste0(TnSeq_folder, "/RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_TAsites.csv"), col_types = cols(...1 = col_skip())) #only need to do if mariner transposon
        colnames(Possible_sites) = c("sites")

        #compute expected dataset
        for (i in 1:num_expected) {
          expected <- data.frame(Pos = sample(Possible_sites$sites, numsites), Exp = sample(sites2$mean, numsites)) %>% arrange(Pos) 
                colnames(expected)[2] <- paste("Expected", i, sep = ".")
                counts.df <- merge(counts.df, expected, by = "Pos", all = T) %>% arrange(Pos)
                counts.df[is.na(counts.df)] <- 0
        }
        rownames(counts.df) <- counts.df$Pos
        counts.norm <- as.matrix(counts.df[,(2:length(counts.df))])
        
        write(paste("\n", "numreads: ", numreads, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("numsites: ", numsites, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)

        # Initialize the lists of read counts per gene and number of independent Tn sites per gene
        controlreps <- 0
        expreps <- 0
        for (c in 1:length(counts.norm[1,])) {
                gff[,c + 9] <- rep(1,length(gff[,1]))
                if (controlreps < ctrl_reps) {
                        controlreps <- controlreps + 1
                        colnames(gff)[c + 9] <- paste(ctrl_pfx, controlreps, sep = ".")
                }
                else {
                        expreps <- expreps + 1
                        colnames(gff)[c + 9] <- paste("Expected", expreps, sep = ".")
                }
        }

        # Output gene boundaries and read counts per Tn site for Perl binning script
        boundariesfile <- paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".boundaries.tsv", sep = ""), sep = "")
        sitecountsfile <- paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".sitecounts.tsv", sep = ""), sep = "")
        write.table(gff[,c(4,5, 10:length(gff))], boundariesfile, quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = F)
        write.table(counts.df, sitecountsfile, quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = F)

Binning reads into genes

Teh TnGeneBin.pl script takes in the gene boundaries and read counts per site and outputs read counts per gene and number of sites per gene. The output files will be used for the DESeq2 analysis below.

myPath="data/TnSeq/"
cd $myPath
    
#input       
perl scripts/TnGeneBin.pl sites/sites_input/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_input_EssentialGenes_100.boundaries.tsv sites/sites_input/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_input_EssentialGenes_100.sitecounts.tsv  
#lac      
perl scripts/TnGeneBin.pl sites/sites_lac/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_lac_EssentialGenes_100.boundaries.tsv sites/sites_lac/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_lac_EssentialGenes_100.sitecounts.tsv

Identifing essential genes with DESeq2

This code chunk reads in the output from the binning script, performs DESeq2 analysis to identify essential genes, and outputs the results. It also outputs summary statistics about the number of significant genes to a text file. It also needs to be runned separately for each sample type (input and lac) based on the mydata and ctrl_pfx variables defined above.

genecounts <- read.table(paste(boundariesfile, "out", sep = "."), header = T)[,-c(1,2)]
        genecounts2 <- read.table(paste(boundariesfile, "out", sep = "."), header = T)
        numsites <- read.table(paste(boundariesfile, "numsites.out", sep = "."), header = T)[,-c(1,2)]

        genes <- data.frame(id = rep("", length(gff[,1]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
        genes$id <- as.character(genes$id)
        for (i in 1:length(gff[,1])) {
                genes$id[i] <- strsplit(grep("locus_tag",strsplit(as.character(gff$att[i]),";")[[1]], value = T),"=")[[1]][2]
                genes$name[i] <- strsplit(grep("Name",strsplit(as.character(gff$att[i]),";")[[1]], value = T),"=")[[1]][2]

        }

        write.table(genecounts2, paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".genecounts2.tsv", sep = ""), sep = ""), quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE)
        
        # Perform differential fitness analysis
        colnames(numsites) <- colnames(gff)[10:length(gff)] #change this number based on number of columns in gff
        numsitesout <- data.frame(numsites[,(1:ctrl_reps)])
        numsitesout[,ctrl_reps + 1] <- rowMeans(numsites[,-(1:ctrl_reps)])
        colnames(numsitesout)[ctrl_reps + 1] <- "Expected_numsites"
        colData <- data.frame(c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps), rep("Expected", num_expected)), condition = c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps),rep("Expected", num_expected)))
        numcountsout <- data.frame(genecounts2[,1:(ctrl_reps + 2)])
        numcountsout[,ctrl_reps + 3] <- rowMeans(genecounts2[,-(1:(ctrl_reps + 2))])
        colnames(numcountsout)[ctrl_reps + 3] <- "Expected_numreads"

        
        ###betaPrior=TRUE
        genescds_T <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = round(genecounts), colData = colData, design = ~ condition)
        #genescds_T <- newCountDataSet(round(genecounts), c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps), rep("Expected", num_expected)))
        #genescds_T$sizeFactor <- rep(1, length(genecounts[1,])) # This is manually set as 1 because we normalized by site above
        genescds_T <- estimateSizeFactors(genescds_T)
        genescds_T <- estimateDispersions(genescds_T) #can set fitType="local" if getting flag at this step for some samples
        genescds_T <- nbinomWaldTest(genescds_T, betaPrior = TRUE)
        res_T <- results(genescds_T, cooksCutoff = FALSE, contrast = c("condition", ctrl_pfx, "Expected"))
        print(head(res_T)) 
        #colnames(res_T)[4] <- paste(ctrl_pfx, "Mean", sep = "")
        #colnames(res_T)[3] <- "ExpectedMean"
        out_T <- cbind(res_T, genes$id, genes$name, numcountsout, numsitesout) # Uncomment if you have a kegg annotation
        #out_T <- cbind(res_T, genes[,2:3], numsitesout) %>% tbl_df # Uncomment if you do not have a kegg annotation
        colnames(out_T)[2] <- "log2FoldChange_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[3] <- "lfcSE_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[4] <- "stat_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[5] <- "pvalue_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[6] <- "padj_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[7] <- "id"

        #Count number of significant genes; can adjust pvalue and padj here based on output(s) that you are interested in
        write(paste("\n", "Analyses with betaPrior = TRUE (l2fc shrinkage):", sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste(paste("\n", "number of genes with pvalue <= 0.01 in TRUE:", sep = ""), sum(res_T$pvalue <= .01, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("number of genes with padj <= 0.01 in TRUE:", sum(res_T$padj <= .01, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        
        # Perform bimodal clustering and essentiality calling and output results
        library(mclust)
        fit_T <- Mclust(out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE, G = 1:2, modelNames = "V")
        summary(fit_T, parameters = TRUE)
        category_T <- rep("",length(out_T$id))
        for (i in 1:length(out_T$id)) {
          if (fit_T$classification[i] == 1 & out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE[i] < 0) {
            category_T[i] <- "Reduced"
          }
          else {
            category_T[i] <- "Unchanged"
          }
        }
        
        density_T <- densityMclust(out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE, G = 1:2, modelNames = "V")
        summary(density_T)
        pdf(paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "TRUE_histogram.pdf", sep = "_"), sep = ""), width = 10, height = 4)
        par(mfrow = c(1,2))
        plot(density_T, what = "density", data = out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE, breaks = 100)
        plot(fit_T, what = "uncertainty")
        dev.off()
        
        #Count number of unchanged and reduced genes, l2fc means, and l2fc variances
        write(paste(paste("\n", "Mclust Unchanged TRUE:", sep = ""), sum(category_T == "Unchanged")), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Mclust Reduced TRUE:", sum(category_T == "Reduced")), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste(paste("\n","Mclust_reduced_mean_TRUE: ", sep = ""), fit_T$parameters$mean[1], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Mclust_unchanged_mean_TRUE: ", fit_T$parameters$mean[2], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste(paste("\n", "Mclust_reduced_variance_TRUE: ", sep = ""), fit_T$parameters$variance$sigmasq[1], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Mclust_unchanged_variance_TRUE: ", fit_T$parameters$variance$sigmasq[2], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        
        fit_T$uncertainty[which(out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE > 0)] <- 0
        print(head(category_T, 10))
        essentiality_T <- as.data.frame(cbind(category_T, fit_T$uncertainty))
        colnames(essentiality_T) <- c("Essentiality_TRUE", "Uncertainty_TRUE")
        out_T <- cbind(out_T, essentiality_T) 
        
        #Count number of significant genes with reduced Mclust; can adjust padj (or pvalue) and uncertaintly values here based on output(s) that you are interested in
        write(paste("\n", "Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.01 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.01)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.01 and padj <=0.01 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & out_T$padj_TRUE <= .01 & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.01, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("\n", "Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.05 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.05)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.05 and padj <=0.05 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & out_T$padj_TRUE <= .05 & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        
        write.csv(out_T, file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".TRUE.DESeq.csv", sep = ""), sep = ""), row.names = T)
Overview
Essential Genes Analysis
Source Code
---
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
format:
  html:
    suppress-bibliography: true
---

# Tn-Seq Pipeline {.unnumbered .unlisted}

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(Biostrings)
library(DESeq2)
library(mclust)
```

This pipeline is based on previously work published by Dr. Ibberson's [@lewin2019; @ibberson2017]. The code in this notebook is adapted with some modifications to fit our data and analysis needs. The original resources can be found here:

-   [Generating a TA sites file](https://ibberson-lab.slab.com/public/posts/generating-a-ta-sites-file-8zr5148f)
-   [Tn-seq Mapping](https://ibberson-lab.slab.com/public/posts/tn-seq-mapping-4kwo7evd)
-   [Essential Gene Analysis](https://ibberson-lab.slab.com/public/posts/essential-gene-analysis-qsa5i4y1)
-   Additional scripts can be found [here](https://github.com/glew8/Tn-seq_co-infection_analyses), including some dependency scripts like the TnGeneBin.pl script for binning reads into genes as part of the essential gene analysis.

```{r}
FOLDER_PATH <- Sys.getenv("BOX_PATH")
TnSeq_folder <- file.path(FOLDER_PATH, "Tn-Seq")
```

## Reference genome and TA sites

We used *Staphylococcus aureus subsp. aureus* USA300_FPR3757 [GCF_000013465.1](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/assembly/GCF_000013465.1/) as reference genome. The assembly was downloaded from NCBI on 12/21/23. Curated versions of the assembly were manually created containing only the main chromosome **NC_007793.1** (the 3 plasmids were removed) and saved as **Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta** and **Sau_USA300_FPR3757.gff** in `data/TnSeq/RefSeqs`.

```{r}
gff_pfx = "Sau_USA300_FPR3757"
gff_dir = file.path(TnSeq_folder, "RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757.gff")
genome_dir <- file.path(TnSeq_folder,"RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta")
```

**Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta** was indexed for use with bowtie2 later in the pipeline

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#conda activate Tn-Seq
bowtie2-build data/TnSeq/RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757.fasta data/TnSeq/RefSeqs/USA300
```

For Mariner transposons, which only insert in TA sites, it is beneficial to identify all of the TA sites and restrict to these sites for downstream analysis. To generate a .csv file of all of the TA sites in a genome, the following code was used:

```{r}
genome <- readDNAStringSet(genome_dir)
ta_sites <- vmatchPattern("TA", genome)
ta_vect <- unname(unlist(start(ta_sites)))
ta_df <- data_frame(sites = ta_vect)
write.csv(ta_df$sites, file.path(TnSeq_folder, "RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_TAsites.csv"))
```

## Tn-seq Mapping

This section is based in the [TnSeq2.sh](https://ibberson-lab.slab.com/public/posts/tn-seq-mapping-4kwo7evd) script. This section processes the raw fastq files, maps the reads to the reference genome, and tallies the mapping results to identify insertion sites and read counts per site.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
myPath="data/TnSeq/"

mkdir -p $myPath/logs
mkdir -p $myPath/bam
mkdir -p $myPath/sites
mkdir -p $myPath/genes

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.fastq.gz; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "Initializing TnSeq analysis on $PREFIX"
    echo "TnSeq processing stats for $PREFIX" > $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    echo "Number of raw reads: " >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    zcat $file | grep -c '^@' >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done
```

### Trimming of IRs & Adaptors

This code chunk removes adapters by removing every sequence before the IR sequence using cutadapt [@martin2011]. The number of reads post-trimming is logged for each sample. The `-m 20` option ensures that only reads of at least 20 bp after trimming are retained, which helps to filter out very short reads that may not be informative for mapping.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
myPath="data/TnSeq/"

#conda activate Tn-Seq
IR="GCCAACCTGTTA"

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.fastq.gz; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "$PREFIX: Removing primer and IR sequences..."
    cutadapt -g $IR -m 20 --discard-untrimmed -o $myPath/fastq/$PREFIX.trim.fastq $file > $myPath/logs/$PREFIX.cutadapt_log.txt
    echo "Number of post-trimmed reads: " >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    grep -c '^@' $myPath/fastq/$PREFIX.trim.fastq >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done

```

Some of the samples were resequenced, so we had to concatenate the trim files for the original and resequenced samples before moving forward with the analysis.

```{bash}
myPath="data/TnSeq/"

cd $myPath/fastq
cat 5772_021_S19_R1_001.trim.fastq 5772_021reseq_S1_R1_001.trim.fastq > 5772_021_S01_R1_001.trim.fastq
cat 5772_022_S20_R1_001.trim.fastq 5772_022reseq_S2_R1_001.trim.fastq > 5772_022_S00_R1_001.trim.fastq
cat 5772_027_S1_R1_001.trim.fastq 5772_027_S61_R1_001.trim.fastq > 5772_027_S00_R1_001.trim.fastq
```

### Mapping to reference genome

This code chunk maps the reads to the reference genome using Bowtie2 [@Langmead2012; @Langmead2019] and processes the resulting SAM files with samtools [@Danecek2021] to generate sorted BAM files containing only mapped, primary alignments. The mapping report from Bowtie2 and the number of mapped reads are logged for each sample.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
myPath="data/TnSeq/"
GENOME=$myPath"RefSeqs/USA300"

#conda activate Tn-Seq

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.trim.fastq; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "$PREFIX: Mapping with Bowtie2..."
    echo "Bowtie2 report:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    bowtie2 --end-to-end -p 16 -a -x $GENOME -U $myPath/fastq/$PREFIX.trim.fastq 2>> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt | \
    samtools view -b -F 4 -F 2048 | samtools sort -@ 16 -o $myPath/bam/$PREFIX-mapped.bam
    echo "Number of mapped reads:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    samtools view -c $myPath/bam/$PREFIX-mapped.bam >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done
```

### Tallying mapping results

This code chunk processes the mapped BAM files to identify unique insertion sites and count the number of reads mapping to each site. The results are saved in text files for each sample, and summary statistics about the number of insertion sites and the most frequent sites are logged.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
myPath="data/TnSeq/"

#conda activate Tn-Seq

for file in $myPath/fastq/*.trim.fastq; do
    PREFIX=`basename ${file%.*.*}`
    echo "$PREFIX: Tallying mapping results..."
    samtools view -F 256 $myPath/bam/$PREFIX-mapped.bam | \
        awk '{if(($2) != 16) print $4; else print $4+length($10)}' | \
        sort | uniq -c | sort -k1nr \
        > $myPath/sites/$PREFIX-sites.txt
    echo "Number of insertion sites identified:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    wc -l $myPath/sites/$PREFIX-sites.txt >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    echo "Most frequent sites:" >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
    head -10 $myPath/sites/$PREFIX-sites.txt >> $myPath/logs/$PREFIX-TnSeq.txt
done
```

## Essential Gene Analysis

This section is based on the [Essential Gene Analysis](https://ibberson-lab.slab.com/public/posts/essential-gene-analysis-qsa5i4y1) script. The code in this section reads in the site counts generated in the previous section, generates expected datasets with the same number of sites and reads, bins the sites into genes using the `TnGeneBin.pl` script, and performs DESeq2 analysis to identify essential genes.

### Counting reads per site and generating expected datasets

We manually made subfolders to separates the sites files for each sample type (input and lac) so that we could run the following code separately for each sample type. The code chunk below is where you select which sample type to run the analysis on. You will need to update the `ctrl_pfx` variable based on the sample type you are running (e.g. "input" or "lac") as this is used in the naming of output files and summary stats.

```{r}
# Run this code chunk separately for each sample type, updating the mydata and ctrl_pfx variables accordingly
mydata <- file.path(TnSeq_folder, "sites/sites_input")
ctrl_pfx = paste(gff_pfx, "input", sep = "_") #update name based on sample type

#mydata <- file.path(TnSeq_folder, "sites/sites_lac")
#ctrl_pfx = paste(gff_pfx, "lac", sep = "_") #update name based on sample type
```

This code chunk reads in the site counts for each sample, generates expected datasets with the same number of sites and reads, and outputs the read counts per site and gene boundaries for the binning script. It also outputs summary statistics about the number of sites and reads to a text file.

```{r}
filenames <- dir(mydata, pattern = ".*-sites.txt", full.names = TRUE)
conditions <- sub("-sites.txt", "", filenames)

ctrl_reps = length(filenames)
num_expected = 100
out_pfx = paste(ctrl_pfx, "EssentialGenes_100", sep = "_")
to_trim = 0

# create summary stats file to record summary info

write(out_pfx, file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""))
write(paste( "\n", "input files:", sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(filenames, file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(paste( "\n", "gff: ", gff_pfx, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(paste("\n", "to trim: ", to_trim, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
write(paste("number of expected datasets: ", num_expected, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)

# Read in sites files
        sites <- data.frame(Pos = c(0)) 
        for (i in 1:length(conditions)) {
                newsites <- read.table(paste(paste(conditions[i], sep = "/"), "sites.txt", sep = "-")) 
                colnames(newsites) <- c(paste("V", i, sep = ""), "Pos")
                newsites <- arrange(newsites,Pos)
                sites <- merge(sites, newsites, all = T) 
        }
        sites <- tail(sites, n = -1)
        sites[is.na(sites)] <- 0

        #output number of total and shared sites across samples. Need to edit based on number of samples.
        sites_total <- sites %>% mutate(numreps = (V1 > 0) + (V2 > 0)) %>% filter(numreps >= 1)
        write(paste("number of sites:", nrow(sites_total)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        sites_shared <- sites %>% mutate(numreps = (V1 > 0) + (V2 > 0)) %>% filter(numreps >= 2)
        write(paste("number of sites shared by all samples:", nrow(sites_shared)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)

        # Normalize data by reads/site
        colData <- data.frame(c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps)), condition = rep("treated", ctrl_reps))
        sitescds <- sites[,2:length(sites)] %>% round %>% DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(colData = colData, design = ~ 1)
        sitescds <- estimateSizeFactors(sitescds)
        #Output the normalized counts
        counts.norm <- counts(sitescds, normalized = F)
        rownames(counts.norm) <- sites$Pos

        # Initialize the list of genes, determine genome length
        #can add column names if your gff has extra columns
        gff <- read.delim(gff_dir, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, skip = 9, header = FALSE, col.names = c("seqname", "source", "feature", "start", "end", "score", "strand", "frame", "att"))
        #change this value if your gff file contains anything besides genes that you want to read in
        gff <- gff[(gff$feature == "gene"),]

        # Initialize read counts per gene and number of independent Tn sites per gene
        # Generate pseudo-datasets with the same number of insertion sites and total reads mapping to those sites, randomly distributed across the genome
        counts.df <- data.frame(counts.norm)
        counts.df$Pos <- as.numeric(rownames(counts.df))
        numreads <- sum(counts.norm)/ctrl_reps
        numsites <- length(which(counts.norm > 0))/ctrl_reps
        
        #generates a new sites data frame and calculates the mean counts for each site in a new column. This column (containing the mean) is then used to sample from to generate the expected datasets.
        sites2 <- sites[2:(ctrl_reps + 1)]
        sites2$mean <- rowMeans(sites2) %>% round

        #For mariner transposon, define Possible_sites
        Possible_sites <- read_csv(paste0(TnSeq_folder, "/RefSeqs/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_TAsites.csv"), col_types = cols(...1 = col_skip())) #only need to do if mariner transposon
        colnames(Possible_sites) = c("sites")

        #compute expected dataset
        for (i in 1:num_expected) {
          expected <- data.frame(Pos = sample(Possible_sites$sites, numsites), Exp = sample(sites2$mean, numsites)) %>% arrange(Pos) 
                colnames(expected)[2] <- paste("Expected", i, sep = ".")
                counts.df <- merge(counts.df, expected, by = "Pos", all = T) %>% arrange(Pos)
                counts.df[is.na(counts.df)] <- 0
        }
        rownames(counts.df) <- counts.df$Pos
        counts.norm <- as.matrix(counts.df[,(2:length(counts.df))])
        
        write(paste("\n", "numreads: ", numreads, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("numsites: ", numsites, sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)

        # Initialize the lists of read counts per gene and number of independent Tn sites per gene
        controlreps <- 0
        expreps <- 0
        for (c in 1:length(counts.norm[1,])) {
                gff[,c + 9] <- rep(1,length(gff[,1]))
                if (controlreps < ctrl_reps) {
                        controlreps <- controlreps + 1
                        colnames(gff)[c + 9] <- paste(ctrl_pfx, controlreps, sep = ".")
                }
                else {
                        expreps <- expreps + 1
                        colnames(gff)[c + 9] <- paste("Expected", expreps, sep = ".")
                }
        }

        # Output gene boundaries and read counts per Tn site for Perl binning script
        boundariesfile <- paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".boundaries.tsv", sep = ""), sep = "")
        sitecountsfile <- paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".sitecounts.tsv", sep = ""), sep = "")
        write.table(gff[,c(4,5, 10:length(gff))], boundariesfile, quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = F)
        write.table(counts.df, sitecountsfile, quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = F)
```

### Binning reads into genes

Teh `TnGeneBin.pl` script takes in the gene boundaries and read counts per site and outputs read counts per gene and number of sites per gene. The output files will be used for the DESeq2 analysis below.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
myPath="data/TnSeq/"
cd $myPath
    
#input       
perl scripts/TnGeneBin.pl sites/sites_input/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_input_EssentialGenes_100.boundaries.tsv sites/sites_input/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_input_EssentialGenes_100.sitecounts.tsv  
#lac      
perl scripts/TnGeneBin.pl sites/sites_lac/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_lac_EssentialGenes_100.boundaries.tsv sites/sites_lac/Sau_USA300_FPR3757_lac_EssentialGenes_100.sitecounts.tsv 
```

### Identifing essential genes with DESeq2

This code chunk reads in the output from the binning script, performs DESeq2 analysis to identify essential genes, and outputs the results. It also outputs summary statistics about the number of significant genes to a text file. It also needs to be runned separately for each sample type (input and lac) based on the `mydata` and `ctrl_pfx` variables defined above.

```{r}
genecounts <- read.table(paste(boundariesfile, "out", sep = "."), header = T)[,-c(1,2)]
        genecounts2 <- read.table(paste(boundariesfile, "out", sep = "."), header = T)
        numsites <- read.table(paste(boundariesfile, "numsites.out", sep = "."), header = T)[,-c(1,2)]

        genes <- data.frame(id = rep("", length(gff[,1]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
        genes$id <- as.character(genes$id)
        for (i in 1:length(gff[,1])) {
                genes$id[i] <- strsplit(grep("locus_tag",strsplit(as.character(gff$att[i]),";")[[1]], value = T),"=")[[1]][2]
                genes$name[i] <- strsplit(grep("Name",strsplit(as.character(gff$att[i]),";")[[1]], value = T),"=")[[1]][2]

        }

        write.table(genecounts2, paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".genecounts2.tsv", sep = ""), sep = ""), quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE)
        
        # Perform differential fitness analysis
        colnames(numsites) <- colnames(gff)[10:length(gff)] #change this number based on number of columns in gff
        numsitesout <- data.frame(numsites[,(1:ctrl_reps)])
        numsitesout[,ctrl_reps + 1] <- rowMeans(numsites[,-(1:ctrl_reps)])
        colnames(numsitesout)[ctrl_reps + 1] <- "Expected_numsites"
        colData <- data.frame(c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps), rep("Expected", num_expected)), condition = c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps),rep("Expected", num_expected)))
        numcountsout <- data.frame(genecounts2[,1:(ctrl_reps + 2)])
        numcountsout[,ctrl_reps + 3] <- rowMeans(genecounts2[,-(1:(ctrl_reps + 2))])
        colnames(numcountsout)[ctrl_reps + 3] <- "Expected_numreads"

        
        ###betaPrior=TRUE
        genescds_T <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = round(genecounts), colData = colData, design = ~ condition)
        #genescds_T <- newCountDataSet(round(genecounts), c(rep(ctrl_pfx, ctrl_reps), rep("Expected", num_expected)))
        #genescds_T$sizeFactor <- rep(1, length(genecounts[1,])) # This is manually set as 1 because we normalized by site above
        genescds_T <- estimateSizeFactors(genescds_T)
        genescds_T <- estimateDispersions(genescds_T) #can set fitType="local" if getting flag at this step for some samples
        genescds_T <- nbinomWaldTest(genescds_T, betaPrior = TRUE)
        res_T <- results(genescds_T, cooksCutoff = FALSE, contrast = c("condition", ctrl_pfx, "Expected"))
        print(head(res_T)) 
        #colnames(res_T)[4] <- paste(ctrl_pfx, "Mean", sep = "")
        #colnames(res_T)[3] <- "ExpectedMean"
        out_T <- cbind(res_T, genes$id, genes$name, numcountsout, numsitesout) # Uncomment if you have a kegg annotation
        #out_T <- cbind(res_T, genes[,2:3], numsitesout) %>% tbl_df # Uncomment if you do not have a kegg annotation
        colnames(out_T)[2] <- "log2FoldChange_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[3] <- "lfcSE_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[4] <- "stat_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[5] <- "pvalue_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[6] <- "padj_TRUE"
        colnames(out_T)[7] <- "id"

        #Count number of significant genes; can adjust pvalue and padj here based on output(s) that you are interested in
        write(paste("\n", "Analyses with betaPrior = TRUE (l2fc shrinkage):", sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste(paste("\n", "number of genes with pvalue <= 0.01 in TRUE:", sep = ""), sum(res_T$pvalue <= .01, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("number of genes with padj <= 0.01 in TRUE:", sum(res_T$padj <= .01, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        
        # Perform bimodal clustering and essentiality calling and output results
        library(mclust)
        fit_T <- Mclust(out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE, G = 1:2, modelNames = "V")
        summary(fit_T, parameters = TRUE)
        category_T <- rep("",length(out_T$id))
        for (i in 1:length(out_T$id)) {
          if (fit_T$classification[i] == 1 & out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE[i] < 0) {
            category_T[i] <- "Reduced"
          }
          else {
            category_T[i] <- "Unchanged"
          }
        }
        
        density_T <- densityMclust(out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE, G = 1:2, modelNames = "V")
        summary(density_T)
        pdf(paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "TRUE_histogram.pdf", sep = "_"), sep = ""), width = 10, height = 4)
        par(mfrow = c(1,2))
        plot(density_T, what = "density", data = out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE, breaks = 100)
        plot(fit_T, what = "uncertainty")
        dev.off()
        
        #Count number of unchanged and reduced genes, l2fc means, and l2fc variances
        write(paste(paste("\n", "Mclust Unchanged TRUE:", sep = ""), sum(category_T == "Unchanged")), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Mclust Reduced TRUE:", sum(category_T == "Reduced")), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste(paste("\n","Mclust_reduced_mean_TRUE: ", sep = ""), fit_T$parameters$mean[1], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Mclust_unchanged_mean_TRUE: ", fit_T$parameters$mean[2], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste(paste("\n", "Mclust_reduced_variance_TRUE: ", sep = ""), fit_T$parameters$variance$sigmasq[1], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Mclust_unchanged_variance_TRUE: ", fit_T$parameters$variance$sigmasq[2], sep = ""), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        
        fit_T$uncertainty[which(out_T$log2FoldChange_TRUE > 0)] <- 0
        print(head(category_T, 10))
        essentiality_T <- as.data.frame(cbind(category_T, fit_T$uncertainty))
        colnames(essentiality_T) <- c("Essentiality_TRUE", "Uncertainty_TRUE")
        out_T <- cbind(out_T, essentiality_T) 
        
        #Count number of significant genes with reduced Mclust; can adjust padj (or pvalue) and uncertaintly values here based on output(s) that you are interested in
        write(paste("\n", "Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.01 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.01)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.01 and padj <=0.01 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & out_T$padj_TRUE <= .01 & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.01, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("\n", "Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.05 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.05)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        write(paste("Reduced and Uncertainty <=0.05 and padj <=0.05 if TRUE:", sum(out_T$Essentiality_TRUE == "Reduced" & out_T$padj_TRUE <= .05 & as.numeric(as.character(out_T$Uncertainty_TRUE)) <= 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)), file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, "_stats.txt", sep = ""), sep = ""), append = TRUE)
        
        write.csv(out_T, file = paste(mydata, "/", paste(out_pfx, ".TRUE.DESeq.csv", sep = ""), sep = ""), row.names = T)
```
